notes.content: UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals. Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.
notes.contentHtml:
UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

notes.content: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR The following fundamentals course: THEA 50: Fundamentals of Theater Production One course from the following series: THEA 61A: Ancient and Medieval Drama THEA 61B: Drama from the Renaissance to the Modern Age THEA 61C: The Birth of the Modern: Drama and Performance After the Renaissance Plus one course in the student's area of focus chosen from the following: THEA 10: Introduction to Theater Design and Technology THEA 20: Introductory Studies in Acting THEA 21: Acting Studio I: Psychological Realism THEA 30: Introduction to Dance Theory and Technique THEA 31A: Dance Studio I: Asian or Asian Diasporic Practice THEA 31B: Dance Studio I: Ballet THEA 31C: Dance Studio I: Contemporary Dance Theory and Technique THEA 33C: Dance Studio I THEA 36: Introduction to Dance Making THEA 37: African Dance THEA 40: Introduction to Directing THEA 80Z: Indian Dance Please visit the Performance, Play & Design website to learn more about this minor. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Theater Arts Minor Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR


The following fundamentals course:

  • THEA 50: Fundamentals of Theater Production


One course from the following series:

  • THEA 61A: Ancient and Medieval Drama
  • THEA 61B: Drama from the Renaissance to the Modern Age
  • THEA 61C: The Birth of the Modern: Drama and Performance After the Renaissance


Plus one course in the student's area of focus chosen from the following:

  • THEA 10: Introduction to Theater Design and Technology
  • THEA 20: Introductory Studies in Acting
  • THEA 21: Acting Studio I: Psychological Realism
  • THEA 30: Introduction to Dance Theory and Technique
  • THEA 31A: Dance Studio I: Asian or Asian Diasporic Practice
  • THEA 31B: Dance Studio I: Ballet
  • THEA 31C: Dance Studio I: Contemporary Dance Theory and Technique
  • THEA 33C: Dance Studio I
  • THEA 36: Introduction to Dance Making
  • THEA 37: African Dance
  • THEA 40: Introduction to Directing
  • THEA 80Z: Indian Dance



Please visit the Performance, Play & Design website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Theater Arts Minor Program Statement
